none: TARP tunnel component, Lower Des Plaines : environmental impact statement.

The Tunnel and Reservoir Plan (TARP) proposed by the Metropolitan Sanitary District of Greater Chicago aims to improve water quality and manage flooding in the Chicago area. The plan involves constructing a series of tunnels and reservoirs to capture and treat urban runoff, reducing pollution in local waterways and preventing overflows into Lake Michigan. The project is expected to enhance the environment and public health by meeting water quality standards and reducing flood risks. However, funding uncertainties and potential environmental impacts during construction, such as noise and waste disposal, are concerns.
TARP tunnel component, Lower Des Plaines : environmental impact statement.

The Tunnel and Reservoir Plan (TARP) proposed by the Metropolitan Sanitary District of Greater Chicago aims to improve water quality and manage flooding in the Chicago area. The plan involves constructing a series of tunnels and reservoirs to capture and treat urban runoff, reducing pollution in local waterways and preventing overflows into Lake Michigan. The project is expected to enhance the environment and public health by meeting water quality standards and reducing flood risks. However, funding uncertainties and potential environmental impacts during construction, such as noise and waste disposal, are concerns.
Lower Des Plaines, Cook County, Illinois
Metropolitan Sanitary District of Greater Chicago

The Tunnel and Reservoir Plan (TARP) was proposed to address significant flooding and water quality issues in the Chicago area. Public pushback primarily revolved around concerns about construction impacts and funding uncertainties. Despite these challenges, TARP has seen progress in its implementation to improve urban water management.

"The tunnels will capture approximately 90 percent of the pollutant load now discharged during combined-sewer overflows and will reduce the pollutant load 75 percent overall." — Principal Findings Concerning the Effects of the Proposed Action
"Significant earthquake events could adversely affect tunnel alignment and tunnel lining." — Conclusions and Recommendations
